system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight section (along x-axis from x=-50 to x=0)
    // Bottom face (z=-25)
    ( -50  -25  -25)   // 0
    (   0  -25  -25)   // 1
    (   0   25  -25)   // 2
    ( -50   25  -25)   // 3

    // Top face (z=+25)
    ( -50   25   25)   // 4
    (   0   25    25)   // 5
    (   0  -25    25)   // 6
    ( -50  -25    25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section (turning in -y direction)
    // Corner at origin (0,0,z)
    (  25    0   25)   // 8
    (  25    0   -25)   // 9
    (  25  -25   -25)   // 10
    (  25  -25    25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - vertical drop in -y direction
    ( -50  -25   25)   // 12 (already defined as vertex 7)
    ( -50  -75   25)   // 13
    ( -50  -75   -25)   // 14
    ( -50  -25   -25)   // 15 (already defined as vertex 3)

    // Block 3 - horizontal leg in +x direction
    (  25  -75   25)   // 16
    (  25  -75   -25)   // 17
    (  25 -125   -25)   // 18
    (  25 -125    25)   // 19

    // Block 4 - long outlet duct in -x direction
    ( -500  -75   25)   // 20
    ( -500  -75   -25)   // 21
    ( -500 -125   -25)   // 22
    ( -500 -125    25)   // 23
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: inlet straight section
    hex (0 1 2 3 7 6 5 4) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: bend section
    hex (1 8 11 2 6 10 9 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: vertical drop

    // Block 3: horizontal leg
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 4: outlet duct
    hex (16 20 21 17 19 23 22 18) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 7 4 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (20 21 22 23)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 1 2 3)
            (7 6 5 4)

            // Block 1 walls
            (1 8 11 2)
            (6 5 9 10)
            (8 10 9 11)
            (2 11 6 0)

            // Block 2 walls

            (3 7 6 15)
            (14 13 7 15)
            (12 13 14 15)

            // Block 3 walls
            (16 17 19 12)
            (19 18 17 11)
            (12 16 17 13)
            (15 19 18 11)

            // Block 4 walls
            (20 21 22 16)
            (19 23 22 18)
            (16 20 21 17)
            (19 18 22 23)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoSimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (15 14 13 12 3 7 6 0) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(15 12 13 14) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run1/runs/squareBend/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 62.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Block hex `(15 14 13 12 3 7 6 0)` has inward-pointing faces — the block is inside-out/inverted.
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find the mesh ("points" file) — this is a cascading error from blockMesh failing.

## Diagnosis

Let me carefully examine the problematic block: `hex (15 14 13 12 3 7 6 0)` where:
- 15: (-50, -25, 25)
- 14: (-50, -75, 25)
- 13: (-50, -75, -25)
- 12: (-50, -25, -25)
- 3: (-50, 25, 25)
- 7: (0, -25, 25)
- 6: (0, -25, -25)
- 0: (-50, 25, -25)

All vertices have x-coordinate = -50 **except** vertices 7 and 6 which have x=0. So the block spans only in the x-direction from x=-50 to x=0, and in the y/z directions from y=-75..25 and z=-25..25. The issue is the **vertex ordering**.

For a hex block `hex(v0 v1 v2 v3 v4 v5 v6 v7)`, the first four vertices form one face and the last four form the opposite face. The normal of the first face should point from the second face toward the first face.

Face 1: (15 14 13 12) = [(-50,-25,25), (-50,-75,25), (-50,-75,-25), (-50,-25,-25)] — ALL at x=-50, so this face normal points in the **-x** direction.
Face 2: (3 7 6 0) = [(-50,25,25), (0,-25,25), (0,-25,-25), (-50,25,-25)] — this face has mixed x-values.

The problem is that vertices 3 and 0 are at x=-50, same as the first face. This means both faces lie in parallel planes perpendicular to the x-axis, but the second face isn't purely at x=0 — it has vertices at both x=-50 and x=0, making it non-planar relative to a proper opposing face. The block is malformed.

## Root Cause

The vertex numbering scheme throughout the `blockMeshDict` doesn't follow the conventional hex block topology where:
- Vertices 0-3 form one rectangular face
- Vertices 4-7 form the opposite face
- And the block connects them as a prism

The current layout mixes vertices from different logical faces across the two groups. Additionally, the bend geometry has overlapping arms that cause multiple blocks to share vertices in ways that create degenerate or inverted hexahedra.

## Solution

**Redesign the entire `blockMeshDict`** using a proven squareBend vertex layout. The current approach tries to force a complex multi-regional L-shaped duct with vertices crammed into degenerate positions. Instead, simplify to a structure where each hex block has clearly separated, non-coplanar vertex pairs.

Here’s the recommended approach:

1. **Restructure vertices** so that each block has two clearly opposite faces with consistent outward normals. For example, for Block 2 (vertical drop):
   - Change `hex (15 14 13 12 3 7 6 0)` to `hex (12 13 14 15 7 6 2 3)`
   - Face 1: (12 13 14 15) at x=-50, y=-75..-25, z=-25..25 — normal in -x
   - Face 2: (7 6 2 3) at x=-50..0, y=-25, z=-25..25 — normal in +x

2. **Apply the same correction to all blocks**:
   - Block 0: `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` — already fixed from Attempt 1
   - Block 1: `hex (7 6 10 11 3 2 9 8)` — needs reordering to `hex (6 7 11 10 2 3 8 9)`
   - Block 2: `hex (12 13 14 15 7 6 2 3)` — reordered as above
   - Block 3: `hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 11)` — reorder to `hex (12 13 17 16 15 19 18 11)`
   - Block 4: `hex (16 20 21 17 19 23 22 18)` — reorder to `hex (16 17 21 20 19 18 23 22)`

3. **Update all face definitions** in the `boundary` section to match the new block vertex orderings.

Alternatively, and more reliably: **Adopt the exact vertex coordinates and block topology from the official OpenFOAM squareBend tutorial** (found in `$FOAM_TUTORIALS/compressible/rhoSimpleFoam/squareBend`). That tutorial's `blockMeshDict` has been validated and will produce a proper mesh. Copy its vertices, blocks, edges, and boundary definitions verbatim, then apply only the user-specific overrides (timing, BC values, turbulence model, etc.).

## Error 2: Cascading

The `rhoSimpleFoam` error is purely because `blockMesh` never succeeded. Once the mesh is fixed and `blockMesh` runs, this resolves automatically.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire file with a corrected blockMeshDict using properly ordered hex blocks; Redefine vertices so each hex has two non-coplanar opposite faces with consistent outward normals; Define 5 blocks: Block 0 inlet straight (hex 0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7 at x=-50..0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25), Block 1 bend corner (hex 1 8 11 5 2 9 10 6 at x=0..25, y=-25..25, z=-25..25), Block 2 vertical drop (hex 12 13 14 15 7 6 2 3 at x=-50, y=-75..-25, z=-25..25), Block 3 horizontal leg (hex 12 16 17 13 15 19 18 11 at x=-50..25, y=-75, z=-25..25), Block 4 outlet duct (hex 16 20 21 17 19 23 22 18 at x=-500..-50, y=-75..-25, z=-25..25); Fix all block definitions with correct vertex ordering where first four vertices form one face with normal pointing toward the second four vertices; Update boundary patches: inlet gets face (0 4 7 3), outlet gets face (20 21 22 23), walls get all remaining external faces from all 5 blocks; Keep scale 1 and mergePatchPairs empty'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
